chat: Good afternoon, Mr. Reinger, I'm Nurse Reyes. Before we get started, can you tell me if you have any history of heart or lung conditions, or any allergies I should know about?

Clarence Reinger.description: triage: vitals and history @ General Hospital:Triage:triage bay 1
chat: Good afternoon, Mr. Reinger, I'm Nurse Reyes. Before we get started, can you tell me if you have any history of heart or lung conditions, or any allergies I should know about?
"No heart or lung problems, and no allergies that I know of—but I do get urinary tract infections pretty often, so I figured that was worth mentioning."

Nurse Reyes.description: triage: vitals and history @ General Hospital:Triage:triage bay 1
chat: Good afternoon, Mr. Reinger, I'm Nurse Reyes. Before we get started, can you tell me if you have any history of heart or lung conditions, or any allergies I should know about?
"No heart or lung problems, and no allergies that I know of—but I do get urinary tract infections pretty often, so I figured that was worth mentioning."

Clarence Reinger.description: triage: vitals and history @ General Hospital:Triage:triage bay 1
chat: Good afternoon, Mr. Reinger, I'm Nurse Reyes. Before we get started, can you tell me if you have any history of heart or lung conditions, or any allergies I should know about?
"No heart or lung problems, and no allergies that I know of—but I do get urinary tract infections pretty often, so I figured that was worth mentioning."

"Thank you for letting me know, Mr. Reinger—recurrent UTIs are definitely important to track. Now let's get your vitals started; I'm going to check your blood pressure, oxygen levels, and temperature first."

Contractually obligated and genuinely excited. Mostly excited.
That is the right ratio. So, congratulations are in order, from what the intake form says. Tell me the story from the beginning.

Clarence Reinger.description: in the prenatal consult @ General Hospital:OB / Prenatal Clinic:exam room
chat: That is the right ratio. So, congratulations are in order, from what the intake form says. Tell me the story from the beginning.

Okay, so. My period did not come when it was supposed to, and I am usually pretty regular, so after a few days I took a home test. Then I took a second one, because I did not believe the first one. Both had the two lines.
There may have been a third test.

Clarence Reinger.description: in the prenatal consult @ General Hospital:OB / Prenatal Clinic:exam room
chat: There may have been a third test.
There was a third test. Scientific rigor.
Peer-reviewed and replicated, I like it. How have you been feeling since?

Marcus.description: in the prenatal consult @ General Hospital:OB / Prenatal Clinic:exam room
chat: There may have been a third test.
There was a third test. Scientific rigor.
Peer-reviewed and replicated, I like it. How have you been feeling since?

Dr. Amari.description: in the prenatal consult @ General Hospital:OB / Prenatal Clinic:exam room
chat: There may have been a third test.
There was a third test. Scientific rigor.
Peer-reviewed and replicated, I like it. How have you been feeling since?

Clarence Reinger.description: in the prenatal consult @ General Hospital:OB / Prenatal Clinic:exam room
chat: Peer-reviewed and replicated, I like it. How have you been feeling since?

Mostly good? Tired in a way that sneaks up on me — like, sit down on the couch at seven and wake up at nine tired. A little queasy in the mornings if I wait too long to eat. Crackers before I even get up seem to fix it.
Smart. Any vomiting you cannot keep ahead of, any dizziness or fainting?

Clarence Reinger.description: in the prenatal consult @ General Hospital:OB / Prenatal Clinic:exam room
chat: Smart. Any vomiting you cannot keep ahead of, any dizziness or fainting?
No, nothing like that. Just the wooziness if I am hungry.
Very important questions, and I will ask them plainly. Any bleeding or spotting at all? Any cramping or belly pain?

Marcus.description: in the prenatal consult @ General Hospital:OB / Prenatal Clinic:exam room

Clarence Reinger.description: in the prenatal consult @ General Hospital:OB / Prenatal Clinic:exam room
chat: Very important questions, and I will ask them plainly. Any bleeding or spotting at all? Any cramping or belly pain?
No bleeding, no. Maybe the tiniest pulling feeling low down once in a while, like a stretch, but not pain.
That stretching feeling is common and reassuring language you are using. Any burning with urination, going more often than you would expect, fevers?

Marcus.description: in the prenatal consult @ General Hospital:OB / Prenatal Clinic:exam room

Dr. Amari.description: in the prenatal consult @ General Hospital:OB / Prenatal Clinic:exam room
chat: Very important questions, and I will ask them plainly. Any bleeding or spotting at all? Any cramping or belly pain?

Clarence Reinger.description: in the prenatal consult @ General Hospital:OB / Prenatal Clinic:exam room
chat: That stretching feeling is common and reassuring language you are using. Any burning with urination, going more often than you would expect, fevers?

Going more often, yes, but I read that is just pregnancy. No burning. And I am glad you asked, because that is my big worry — I get urinary tract infections all the time. Like, at least a couple every year since my twenties. I know the feeling by heart, and I am scared of what happens if I get one while pregnant.

I am really glad you told me, and it is on your chart too — recurrent urinary tract infections change how carefully we watch. Two things. First, today we send a urine culture no matter what, because in pregnancy an infection can simmer without symptoms, and untreated ones can cause real trouble. Second, that culture becomes routine at your visits. If anything grows, we treat it promptly with medicine that is safe in pregnancy. You will not be left guessing.

Okay. That actually helps a lot. Marcus, breathe.
I am breathing. I wrote it down, that is all.

Clarence Reinger.description: in the prenatal consult @ General Hospital:OB / Prenatal Clinic:exam room
chat: I am breathing. I wrote it down, that is all.
Keep writing, it is a good habit. Now, your medication list is flagged as due for review, so let us do it properly. What do you take — prescriptions, over the counter, anything at all?
Honestly, almost nothing these days. I stopped my birth control a while back when we decided to start trying, and since then it is just the occasional thing for a headache. Nothing regular.

Good — we will update the chart so it reflects reality. From here on, before you take anything new, even over the counter, run it past us. There is always a safe option; it is just sometimes a different one than the shelf suggests.
Got it. Marcus is writing that down too.

Clarence Reinger.description: in the prenatal consult @ General Hospital:OB / Prenatal Clinic:exam room
chat: Got it. Marcus is writing that down too.

Excellent. Let me walk you through what today involves, because the first visit is the big one — lots of boxes to check, all of them routine. A urine pregnancy test here in the office to make it official. A physical exam. An ultrasound to confirm the pregnancy is in the uterus where it belongs and to check viability. I will measure your belly and we will listen for the baby. And then blood work — a whole panel.

I will measure your belly and we will listen for the baby. And then blood work — a whole panel.
How much blood are we talking? I am a fainter. Historically.

Noted — we will have you lying down with juice on standby. It is one draw, several tubes: your blood type, a full blood count to check for anemia, and the standard infection screen we run for every single pregnant patient — hepatitis B, hepatitis C, HIV, and syphilis, plus checking your immunity to rubella and chickenpox. We also test urine for chlamydia and gonorrhea, screen your urine for sugar and protein, and do a Pap smear if you are due — and you are due.

Marcus.description: in the prenatal consult @ General Hospital:OB / Prenatal Clinic:exam room
chat: Excellent. Let me walk you through what today involves, because the first visit is the big one — lots of boxes to check, all of them routine. Clarence Reinger.description: in the prenatal consult @ General Hospital:OB / Prenatal Clinic:exam room
chat: Noted — we will have you lying down with juice on standby. It is one draw, several tubes: your blood type, a full blood count to check for anemia, and the standard infection screen we run for every single pregnant patient — hepatitis B, hepatitis C, HIV, and syphilis, plus checking your immunity to rubella and chickenpox. We also test urine for chlamydia and gonorrhea, screen your urine for sugar and protein, and do a Pap smear if you are due — and you are due.

The whole buffet, once, so we start with a complete picture. None of it implies anything about you personally — every patient, every pregnancy, same list. We will also place a little tuberculosis skin test on your forearm today; someone just has to look at it in two to three days, which the front desk can book as a quick nurse visit.

Can I ask the question we argued about in the car? Coffee. She says one cup is fine, I read a scary blog.
The argument ends in her favor. A regular cup a day is fine. The blog loses. Moderation is the theme for most things — the true no-go list is alcohol, smoking, and anything recreational.

chat: The argument ends in her favor. A regular cup a day is fine. The blog loses. Moderation is the theme for most things — the true no-go list is alcohol, smoking, and anything recreational.
None of those are in the picture. Never smoked, and I have not had a drink since the two lines.
Perfect. Let us do the exam and the ultrasound now, and then we will talk again... All right. So — the office urine test agrees with your three home tests. You are officially pregnant.

chat: Perfect. Let us do the exam and the ultrasound now, and then we will talk again... All right. So — the office urine test agrees with your three home tests. You are officially pregnant.

Exam first: everything looks healthy and normal. Your uterus feels the size I would expect this early — I checked the fundal height, which is how we track growth from here on. And now the part you actually came for. Look at the screen — there. Right in the middle of the uterus, exactly where we want it.

chat: Exam first: everything looks healthy and normal. Your uterus feels the size I would expect this early — I checked the fundal height, which is how we track growth from here on. That little... that is it? That flicker?
That flicker is the heartbeat. Strong and steady. A viable pregnancy, right where it should be.

Clarence Reinger.description: in the prenatal consult @ General Hospital:OB / Prenatal Clinic:exam room
chat: That flicker is the heartbeat. Strong and steady. A viable pregnancy, right where it should be.
Oh wow. Okay. Wow. I did not think it would be — visible. Clarence, look.
I am looking! I am also crying a little, which is very on brand for me lately.

Clarence Reinger.description: in the prenatal consult @ General Hospital:OB / Prenatal Clinic:exam room
chat: I am looking! I am also crying a little, which is very on brand for me lately.
On brand for most people in this room, most weeks. I will print you pictures. I also listened in — the heart sounds are exactly what we want to hear.
So everything is... normal? Like, actually normal? With my UTI history I had convinced myself something would be complicated.

Clarence Reinger.description: in the prenatal consult @ General Hospital:OB / Prenatal Clinic:exam room
chat: So everything is... normal? Like, actually normal? With my UTI history I had convinced myself something would be complicated.

Everything today says normal, healthy pregnancy. The UTI history does not change that — it just means we stay vigilant with the urine cultures, and you call us at the first hint of symptoms rather than waiting. Burning, urgency beyond the usual, back pain on one side, fever — those are call-us-today symptoms, not wait-and-see symptoms.
Call the same day. Marcus has it underlined twice, I assume.

Clarence Reinger.description: in the prenatal consult @ General Hospital:OB / Prenatal Clinic:exam room
chat: Call the same day. Marcus has it underlined twice, I assume.
Three times. What is the schedule from here?

We will see her every four weeks for now, more often later in the pregnancy. The nurse will draw the blood before you leave, place the TB test, and book two things: the quick visit to read the test this week, and your next prenatal visit. Results from today's panel come to your patient portal, and we call about anything that needs action.

Marcus.description: in the prenatal consult @ General Hospital:OB / Prenatal Clinic:exam room

chat: We will see her every four weeks for now, more often later in the pregnancy. And in the meantime I just... live my life? Work, walks, normal food?
Live your life. Eat well, keep the crackers strategy, sleep when the couch claims you, and let the queasiness bully you a little less each week. Any last questions before the blood draw?

chat: Live your life. Eat well, keep the crackers strategy, sleep when the couch claims you, and let the queasiness bully you a little less each week. Any last questions before the blood draw?
Just one. When can we tell people? Is there a rule?
No medical rule — that one is entirely yours to decide, together. Some people wait, some call their mother from the parking lot.
